 25        static string GetHierarchyPath(Transform transform)
 326        {
 327            if (transform == null)
 028                return string.Empty;
 29
 330            var path = new StringBuilder();
 1831            while (transform != null)
 1532            {
 1533                if (path.Length > 0)
 1234                    path.Insert(0, "/");
 1535                path.Insert(0, transform.name);
 1536                transform = transform.parent;
 1537            }
 338            return "/" + path.ToString();
 339        }

 56        /// <summary>
 57        /// Creates a new Search query, optionally matching text content.
 58        /// This is a convenience method - you can also use Search.Name(), Search.Text(), etc. directly.
 59        /// </summary>
 60        /// <param name="textPattern">Optional text pattern to match (searches visible text content).</param>
 61        /// <returns>A new Search instance for chaining conditions.</returns>
 62        /// <example>await Click(Search("Play"));  // finds element with "Play" text</example>
 63        /// <example>await Click(Search().Name("Button*").Type&lt;Button&gt;());  // chain conditions</example>
 64        /// <example>await Click(new Search("Play"));  // text search via constructor</example>
 65        /// <example>await Click(new Search().Type&lt;Button&gt;().Text("Submit"));  // chained</example>
 066        protected static Search Search(string textPattern = null) => new Search(textPattern);
 67
 68        /// <summary>

 70        /// </summary>

 72        /// <returns>A new Search instance for chaining additional conditions.</returns>

 74        /// // Find and click a button by name
 75        /// await Click(Name("PlayButton"));
 76        ///

 79        /// </example>
 080        protected static Search Name(string pattern) => new Search().Name(pattern);

 231        /// <summary>
 232        /// Gets or sets the interval in milliseconds between test actions.
 233        /// Default is 100ms. Increase for slower, more visible test playback.
 234        /// </summary>
 235        /// <example>
 236        /// UITestBehaviour.Interval = 200; // Slower playback
 237        /// </example>
 129238        public static int Interval { get; set; } = 100;
 239
 240        /// <summary>
 241        /// When true, increases all intervals and enables verbose logging for debugging tests.
 242        /// </summary>
 235243        public static bool DebugMode { get; set; } = false;
 244
 245        /// <summary>
 246        /// Multiplier for all intervals when DebugMode is enabled. Default is 3x.
 247        /// </summary>
 1248        public static float DebugIntervalMultiplier { get; set; } = 3f;
 249
 250        /// <summary>
 251        /// Gets the effective interval, accounting for debug mode multiplier.
 252        /// </summary>
 128253        static int EffectiveInterval => DebugMode ? (int)(Interval * DebugIntervalMultiplier) : Interval;

 0258                Debug.Log($"[UITEST:DEBUG] {message}");

 264        /// <summary>

 273        /// <summary>

 275        /// in long-running operations or to pass to async methods that support cancellation.
 276        /// </summary>
 300277        protected static CancellationToken TestCancellationToken => testCts != null ? testCts.Token : CancellationToken.

 279        /// <summary>
 280        /// Registers a custom type as clickable, allowing Click methods to find it.
 281        /// Use this for custom UI systems that don't inherit from Selectable.
 282        /// </summary>
 283        /// <param name="type">The type to register as clickable.</param>
 284        /// <example>
 285        /// UITestBehaviour.RegisterClickable(typeof(MyCustomButton));
 286        /// </example>
 287        public static void RegisterClickable(Type type)
 0288        {
 0289            if (type != null && !clickablesList.Contains(type))
 0290            {
 0291                clickablesList.Add(type);
 0292                clickablesArray = clickablesList.ToArray();
 0293            }
 0294        }
 295
 296        /// <summary>
 297        /// Registers a custom type as clickable using generics.
 298        /// </summary>
 299        /// <typeparam name="T">The type to register as clickable.</typeparam>

 302        /// </example>
 0303        public static void RegisterClickable<T>() => RegisterClickable(typeof(T));

 305        /// <summary>
 306        /// Unregisters a type from being considered clickable.
 307        /// </summary>
 308        /// <param name="type">The type to unregister.</param>
 309        public static void UnregisterClickable(Type type)
 0310        {
 0311            if (clickablesList.Remove(type))
 0312                clickablesArray = clickablesList.ToArray();
 0313        }
 314
 315        /// <summary>

 0319        public static void UnregisterClickable<T>() => UnregisterClickable(typeof(T));

 321        /// <summary>
 322        /// Stops the currently running test by cancelling its cancellation token.
 323        /// Call this to abort a test from outside the test method.
 324        /// </summary>
 325        public static void StopTest()
 0326        {
 0327            if (testCts != null)
 0328                testCts.Cancel();
 0329        }
 330
 331        protected virtual void OnDestroy()
 56332        {
 56333            if (testCts != null && Scenario == testScenario)
 0334            {
 0335                testCts.Cancel();
 0336                testCts.Dispose();
 0337                testCts = null;
 0338            }
 56339        }

 341        /// <summary>
 342        /// Called at the end of each action to wait for the interval before continuing.
 343        /// </summary>
 344        static async UniTask ActionComplete()
 69345        {
 346
 347            // Always yield at least one frame to allow Unity to process events
 207348            await UniTask.Yield(PlayerLoopTiming.Update, TestCancellationToken);
 349
 350            // Then wait for the remaining interval time (uses EffectiveInterval for debug mode)
 69351            int delay = EffectiveInterval;
 69352            LogDebug($"ActionComplete: waiting {delay}ms");
 207353            await UniTask.Delay(delay, true, PlayerLoopTiming.Update, TestCancellationToken);
 69354        }
 355
 356        /// <summary>
 357        /// Waits until the editor is fully in play mode and ready for testing.
 358        /// </summary>
 359        static async UniTask WaitForPlayModeReady()
 0360        {
 361#if UNITY_EDITOR
 362            // Wait until EditorApplication.isPlaying is true and isPlayingOrWillChangePlaymode settles
 0363            while (!UnityEditor.EditorApplication.isPlaying || UnityEditor.EditorApplication.isCompiling)
 0364            {
 0365                await UniTask.Yield(PlayerLoopTiming.Update, TestCancellationToken);
 0366            }
 367#endif
 368            // Wait for Time.timeScale to be non-zero (game not paused)
 0369            while (Time.timeScale == 0)
 0370            {
 0371                await UniTask.Yield(PlayerLoopTiming.Update, TestCancellationToken);
 0372            }
 373
 374            // Yield a few frames to let Unity fully settle
 0375            for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++)
 0376            {
 0377                await UniTask.Yield(PlayerLoopTiming.Update, TestCancellationToken);
 0378            }
 0379        }
 380
 381        /// <summary>
 382        /// Checks if an object meets the availability requirements specified by a Search query.
 383        /// By default (no IncludeInactive/IncludeDisabled), requires active and enabled.
 384        /// </summary>
 385        static bool CheckAvailability(UnityEngine.Object obj, Search search)
 1078386        {
 1078387            if (obj == null)
 0388                return false;
 389
 1078390            GameObject go = null;
 1078391            Behaviour behaviour = null;
 392
 1078393            if (obj is GameObject g)
 0394                go = g;
 1078395            else if (obj is UnityEngine.Component c)
 1078396            {
 1078397                go = c.gameObject;
 1078398                behaviour = c as Behaviour;
 1078399            }
 400
 1078401            if (go == null)
 0402                return false;
 403
 404            // Check active state (unless IncludeInactive is set)
 1078405            if (search == null || !search.ShouldIncludeInactive)
 1078406            {
 1078407                if (!go.activeInHierarchy)
 0408                    return false;
 1078409            }
 410
 411            // Check enabled/interactable state (unless IncludeDisabled is set)
 1078412            if (search == null || !search.ShouldIncludeDisabled)
 1078413            {
 1078414                if (behaviour != null && !behaviour.enabled)
 92415                    return false;
 416
 986417                var canvasGroup = go.GetComponentInParent<CanvasGroup>();
 986418                if (canvasGroup != null && (canvasGroup.alpha <= 0 || !canvasGroup.interactable))
 0419                    return false;
 986420            }
 421
 986422            return true;
 1078423        }

 425        /// <summary>
 426        /// Gets the scenario number for this test from the [UITest] attribute.
 427        /// Throws if the attribute is missing or the scenario is invalid.
 428        /// </summary>
 429        public int Scenario
 430        {
 431            get
 0432            {
 0433                var attr = GetType().GetCustomAttribute<UITestAttribute>();
 0434                if (attr == null)
 0435                    throw new InvalidOperationException($"{GetType().Name} missing [UITest] attribute");
 0436                if (attr.Scenario <= 0)
 0437                    throw new InvalidOperationException($"{GetType().Name} [UITest] Scenario must be > 0");
 0438                return attr.Scenario;
 0439            }
 440        }

 451        /// <summary>

 454        /// </summary>
 455        /// <param name="name">Base name for the screenshot file. Timestamp will be appended.</param>
 456        /// <example>
 457        /// CaptureScreenshot("before_click");
 458        /// await Click("PlayButton");
 459        /// CaptureScreenshot("after_click");
 460        /// </example>
 461        protected void CaptureScreenshot(string name = "screenshot")
 0462        {
 0463            string path = System.IO.Path.Combine(Application.temporaryCachePath, $"{name}_{DateTime.Now.Ticks}.png");
 0464            ScreenCapture.CaptureScreenshot(path);
 0465            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Screenshot: {path}");
 0466        }

 468        /// <summary>

 473        /// <param name="data">The object to serialize to JSON.</param>
 474        /// <example>

 477        protected void AttachJson(string name, object data)

 0479            string json = JsonUtility.ToJson(data, true);
 0480            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Attach JSON '{name}': {json}");
 0481        }

 0495        }

 0511                Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Attach File '{name}': {filePath} ({mimeType})");
 0512            }
 0513        }

 588        void StartTest(bool clearData, string testDataPath, bool debugMode)
 0589        {
 0590            int scenario = Scenario;
 0591            TestRunName = GetType().Name + "_" + DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyyMMdd_HHmmss");
 592
 0593            if (clearData)
 0594            {
 595                try
 0596                {
 0597                    string folder = System.IO.Path.Combine(Application.persistentDataPath, "data");
 0598                    if (Directory.Exists(folder))
 0599                        Directory.Delete(folder, true);
 0600                    PlayerPrefs.DeleteAll();
 0601                    Debug.Log("[UITEST] Cleared test data");
 0602                }
 0603                catch (Exception ex)
 0604                {
 0605                    Debug.LogError($"[UITEST] Failed to clear: {ex.Message}");
 0606                }
 0607            }
 608
 0609            UnityEditor.SessionState.SetBool("GAME_LOOP_TEST", true);
 0610            UnityEditor.SessionState.SetInt("GAME_LOOP_TEST_SCENARIO", scenario);
 0611            UnityEditor.SessionState.SetString("GAME_LOOP_TEST_NAME", TestRunName);
 0612            UnityEditor.SessionState.SetString("GAME_LOOP_TEST_DATA_PATH", testDataPath ?? "");
 0613            UnityEditor.SessionState.SetBool("GAME_LOOP_TEST_DEBUG", debugMode);
 0614            UnityEditor.EditorApplication.isPlaying = true;
 0615        }
 616
 617        string GetTestDataPath()
 0618        {
 0619            string testFolder = System.IO.Path.Combine(Application.dataPath, "UITestBehaviours", "GeneratedTests");
 0620            string testName = GetType().Name;
 621
 0622            if (!Directory.Exists(testFolder))
 0623                return null;
 624
 0625            var directories = Directory.GetDirectories(testFolder, "*", SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly);
 0626            foreach (var dir in directories)
 0627            {
 0628                string zipPath = System.IO.Path.Combine(dir, "testdata.zip");
 0629                if (File.Exists(zipPath))
 0630                {
 0631                    string scriptPath = System.IO.Path.Combine(dir, $"{testName}.cs");
 0632                    if (File.Exists(scriptPath))
 0633                        return zipPath;
 0634                }
 0635            }
 636
 0637            return null;
 0638        }
 639#endif
 640
 641        void PrepareTestData()
 0642        {
 0643            var attr = GetType().GetCustomAttribute<UITestAttribute>();
 0644            var dataMode = attr != null ? attr.DataMode : TestDataMode.Ask;
 645
 0646            if (dataMode == TestDataMode.UseCurrent)
 0647            {
 0648                Debug.Log("[UITEST] DataMode=UseCurrent, using existing data");
 0649                return;
 650            }
 651
 0652            string testDataPath = FindTestData();
 0653            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(testDataPath))
 0654            {
 0655                Debug.Log("[UITEST] No test data found, using existing data");
 0656                return;
 657            }
 658
 0659            string targetPath = System.IO.Path.Combine(Application.persistentDataPath, "data");
 660
 661            try
 0662            {
 0663                if (Directory.Exists(targetPath))
 0664                    Directory.Delete(targetPath, true);
 665
 0666                if (testDataPath.EndsWith(".zip", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
 0667                {
 0668                    System.IO.Compression.ZipFile.ExtractToDirectory(testDataPath, Application.persistentDataPath);
 0669                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Extracted test data from: {testDataPath}");
 0670                }
 0671                else if (Directory.Exists(testDataPath))
 0672                {
 0673                    CopyDirectoryRuntime(testDataPath, targetPath);
 0674                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Copied test data from: {testDataPath}");
 0675                }
 0676            }
 0677            catch (Exception ex)
 0678            {
 0679                Debug.LogError($"[UITEST] Failed to prepare test data: {ex.Message}");
 0680            }
 0681        }
 682
 683        string FindTestData()
 0684        {
 685#if UNITY_EDITOR
 686            // Check if a custom data path was specified via context menu
 0687            string customPath = UnityEditor.SessionState.GetString("GAME_LOOP_TEST_DATA_PATH", "");
 0688            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(customPath))
 0689            {
 0690                UnityEditor.SessionState.EraseString("GAME_LOOP_TEST_DATA_PATH");
 0691                if (File.Exists(customPath) || Directory.Exists(customPath))
 0692                    return customPath;
 0693            }
 694
 0695            string editorPath = GetTestDataPath();
 0696            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(editorPath))
 0697                return editorPath;
 698#endif
 0699            return FindTestDataInStreamingAssets();
 0700        }
 701
 702        string FindTestDataInStreamingAssets()
 0703        {
 0704            string testName = GetType().Name;
 0705            string streamingPath = Application.streamingAssetsPath;
 706
 0707            string zipPath = System.IO.Path.Combine(streamingPath, "UITestData", $"{testName}.zip");
 0708            if (File.Exists(zipPath))
 0709                return zipPath;
 710
 0711            string folderPath = System.IO.Path.Combine(streamingPath, "UITestData", testName);
 0712            if (Directory.Exists(folderPath))
 0713                return folderPath;
 714
 0715            return null;
 0716        }
 717
 718        static void CopyDirectoryRuntime(string sourceDir, string targetDir)
 0719        {
 0720            Directory.CreateDirectory(targetDir);
 721
 0722            foreach (string file in Directory.GetFiles(sourceDir))
 0723            {
 0724                string targetFile = System.IO.Path.Combine(targetDir, System.IO.Path.GetFileName(file));
 0725                File.Copy(file, targetFile, true);
 0726            }
 727
 0728            foreach (string dir in Directory.GetDirectories(sourceDir))
 0729            {
 0730                string targetSubDir = System.IO.Path.Combine(targetDir, System.IO.Path.GetFileName(dir));
 0731                CopyDirectoryRuntime(dir, targetSubDir);
 0732            }
 0733        }
 734
 735        async void Start()
 0736        {
 0737            await UniTask.Yield();
 738
 739#if UNITY_EDITOR
 0740            if (UnityEditor.SessionState.GetBool("GAME_LOOP_TEST", false))
 0741            {
 0742                testScenario = UnityEditor.SessionState.GetInt("GAME_LOOP_TEST_SCENARIO", 0);
 0743                TestRunName = UnityEditor.SessionState.GetString("GAME_LOOP_TEST_NAME", "");
 0744                DebugMode = UnityEditor.SessionState.GetBool("GAME_LOOP_TEST_DEBUG", false);
 0745            }
 746
 0747            UnityEditor.SessionState.SetBool("GAME_LOOP_TEST", false);
 0748            UnityEditor.SessionState.SetBool("GAME_LOOP_TEST_DEBUG", false);
 749#endif
 750
 0751            if (Scenario != 0 && Scenario == testScenario)
 0752            {
 0753                GameObject.DontDestroyOnLoad(this.gameObject);
 0754                EnsureSceneCallbackRegistered();
 755
 0756                PrepareTestData();
 757
 0758                if (testCts != null)
 0759                {
 0760                    testCts.Cancel();
 0761                    testCts.Dispose();
 0762                }
 0763                testCts = new CancellationTokenSource();
 764
 0765                if (DebugMode)
 0766                {
 0767                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Test Start (DEBUG MODE): {GetType().Name}");
 0768                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST:DEBUG] Interval: {Interval}ms x {DebugIntervalMultiplier} = {EffectiveInterval}m
 0769                }
 770                else
 0771                {
 0772                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Test Start: {GetType().Name}");
 0773                }
 774
 775                // Wait for editor to be fully in play mode and scene to initialize
 0776                await WaitForPlayModeReady();
 0777                await UniTask.Delay(1000, true, PlayerLoopTiming.Update, TestCancellationToken);
 778
 0779                float testStartTime = Time.realtimeSinceStartup;
 780                try
 0781                {
 0782                    await Test();
 0783                    float duration = Time.realtimeSinceStartup - testStartTime;
 0784                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Test PASSED: {GetType().Name} Duration: {duration:F2}s");
 0785                }
 0786                catch (OperationCanceledException)
 0787                {
 0788                    float duration = Time.realtimeSinceStartup - testStartTime;
 0789                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Test CANCELLED: {GetType().Name} Duration: {duration:F2}s");
 0790                }
 0791                catch (Exception ex)
 0792                {
 0793                    float duration = Time.realtimeSinceStartup - testStartTime;
 0794                    Debug.LogException(ex);
 0795                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Test FAILED: {GetType().Name} Duration: {duration:F2}s");
 0796                }
 797                finally
 0798                {
 0799                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Test End: {GetType().Name}");
 800
 0801                    if (testCts != null)
 0802                    {
 0803                        testCts.Cancel();
 0804                        testCts.Dispose();
 0805                        testCts = null;
 0806                    }
 807
 0808                    await UniTask.Yield();
 809
 810#if UNITY_EDITOR
 0811                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Stopping play mode...");
 0812                    UnityEditor.EditorApplication.isPlaying = false;
 813#else
 814                    Application.Quit(0);
 815#endif
 0816                }
 0817            }
 818            else
 0819            {
 0820                GameObject.Destroy(this.gameObject);
 0821            }
 0822        }
 823        static bool sceneCallbackRegistered;
 824
 825        static void EnsureSceneCallbackRegistered()
 0826        {
 0827            if (sceneCallbackRegistered)
 0828                return;
 0829            sceneCallbackRegistered = true;
 0830            lastKnownScene = SceneManager.GetActiveScene().name;
 0831            lastSceneChangeTime = Time.realtimeSinceStartup;
 0832            SceneManager.sceneLoaded += OnSceneLoadedStatic;
 0833        }
 834
 835        static void OnSceneLoadedStatic(Scene scene, LoadSceneMode mode)
 0836        {
 0837            if (scene.name != lastKnownScene)
 0838            {
 0839                Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Scene changed: {lastKnownScene} -> {scene.name}");
 0840                lastKnownScene = scene.name;
 0841                lastSceneChangeTime = Time.realtimeSinceStartup;
 0842            }
 0843        }
 844
 845        /// <summary>
 846        /// Gets the test scenario information from Android Test Loop intent.
 847        /// Used for Firebase Test Lab integration on Android.
 848        /// </summary>
 849        /// <param name="test">Output: The scenario number from the test intent.</param>
 850        /// <param name="logFile">Output: The log file path from the test intent.</param>
 851        /// <returns>True if running as a Firebase Test Loop scenario, false otherwise.</returns>
 852        public static bool GetScenario(out int test, out string logFile)
 0853        {
 854
 0855            test = 0;
 0856            logFile = "";
 857
 858#if UNITY_ANDROID && !UNITY_EDITOR
 859
 860            AndroidJavaClass unityPlayer = new AndroidJavaClass("com.unity3d.player.UnityPlayer");
 861            var activity = unityPlayer.GetStatic<AndroidJavaObject>("currentActivity");
 862            var intent = activity.Call<AndroidJavaObject>("getIntent");
 863            string action = intent.Call<string>("getAction");
 864
 865            if (action.Equals("com.google.intent.action.TEST_LOOP"))
 866            {
 867                test = intent.Call<int>("getIntExtra", "scenario", 0);
 868                logFile = intent.Call<string>("getDataString");
 869                return true;
 870
 871            }
 872
 873#endif
 874
 0875            return false;
 876
 0877        }
 878        /// <summary>
 879        /// Override this method to implement your test logic.
 880        /// This is the main entry point for your UI test.
 881        /// </summary>
 882        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the test finishes.</returns>
 883        /// <example>
 884        /// protected override async UniTask Test()
 885        /// {
 886        ///     await Click("PlayButton");
 887        ///     await Wait(1);
 888        ///     await Click("SettingsButton");
 889        ///     Assert(/* condition */, "Expected condition to be true");
 890        /// }
 891        /// </example>
 892        protected abstract UniTask Test();
 893
 894        /// <summary>
 895        /// Asserts that a condition is true. Throws TestException if the condition is false.
 896        /// Use this to verify expected test outcomes.
 897        /// </summary>
 898        /// <param name="condition">The condition to evaluate. Test fails if this is false.</param>
 899        /// <param name="message">The error message to include in the exception if assertion fails.</param>
 900        /// <exception cref="TestException">Thrown when the condition is false.</exception>
 901        /// <example>
 902        /// var button = await Find&lt;Button&gt;(Name("PlayButton"));
 903        /// Assert(button != null, "Play button should exist");
 904        /// Assert(button.interactable, "Play button should be interactable");
 905        /// </example>
 906        protected void Assert(bool condition, string message = "Assertion failed")
 0907        {
 0908            if (!condition)
 0909            {
 0910                throw new TestException(message);
 911            }
 0912        }
 913
 914        /// <summary>
 915        /// Waits for the specified duration before continuing the test.
 916        /// </summary>
 917        /// <param name="seconds">The number of seconds to wait. Default is 1 second.</param>
 918        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes after the specified duration.</returns>
 919        /// <example>
 920        /// await Click("PlayButton");
 921        /// await Wait(2); // Wait 2 seconds for animation
 922        /// await Click("NextButton");
 923        /// </example>
 924        protected async UniTask Wait(float seconds = 1f)
 0925        {
 0926            LogDebug($"Wait: {seconds}s");
 0927            await UniTask.Delay((int)(seconds * 1000), true, PlayerLoopTiming.Update, TestCancellationToken);
 0928            await ActionComplete();
 0929        }
 930
 931        /// <summary>
 932        /// Waits until an element matching the search query appears on screen.
 933        /// Throws TimeoutException if the element is not found within the specified time.
 934        /// </summary>
 935        /// <param name="search">The search query to match elements against.</param>
 936        /// <param name="seconds">Maximum time to wait in seconds. Default is 10 seconds.</param>
 937        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the element is found.</returns>
 938        /// <exception cref="TimeoutException">Thrown when element is not found within timeout.</exception>
 939        /// <example>
 940        /// await Click("LoadGameButton");
 941        /// await Wait(Name("GameScene"), 30); // Wait for game to load
 942        /// </example>
 943        protected async UniTask Wait(Search search, int seconds = 10)
 0944        {
 0945            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Wait ({seconds}s)");
 0946            LogDebug($"Wait: with {seconds}s timeout");
 0947            await Find<MonoBehaviour>(search, true, seconds);
 0948        }
 949
 950        /// <summary>
 951        /// Waits until a custom condition becomes true.
 952        /// Throws TimeoutException if the condition is not met within the specified time.
 953        /// </summary>
 954        /// <param name="condition">A function that returns true when the wait should complete.</param>
 955        /// <param name="seconds">Maximum time to wait in seconds. Default is 60 seconds.</param>
 956        /// <param name="description">A description of the condition for logging purposes.</param>
 957        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the condition is true.</returns>
 958        /// <exception cref="TimeoutException">Thrown when condition is not met within timeout.</exception>
 959        /// <example>
 960        /// // Wait until player health is full
 961        /// await WaitFor(() => player.health >= 100, 30, "player health to reach 100");
 962        ///
 963        /// // Wait until loading is complete
 964        /// await WaitFor(() => loadingScreen == null, 60, "loading to complete");
 965        /// </example>
 966        protected async UniTask WaitFor(Func<bool> condition, float seconds = 60, string description = "condition")
 0967        {
 0968            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] WaitFor ({seconds}) [{description}]");
 0969            LogDebug($"WaitFor: '{description}' with {seconds}s timeout");
 970
 0971            var startTime = Time.realtimeSinceStartup;
 972
 0973            while ((Time.realtimeSinceStartup - startTime) < seconds && Application.isPlaying && !TestCancellationToken.
 0974            {
 0975                if (condition())
 0976                {
 0977                    LogDebug($"WaitFor: '{description}' satisfied after {Time.realtimeSinceStartup - startTime:F2}s");
 0978                    await ActionComplete();
 0979                    return;
 980                }
 981
 0982                await UniTask.Delay(EffectiveInterval, true, PlayerLoopTiming.Update, TestCancellationToken);
 0983            }
 984
 0985            TestCancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
 0986            throw new System.TimeoutException($"Condition '{description}' not met within {seconds} seconds");
 0987        }
 988
 989        /// <summary>
 990        /// Waits until the scene changes from the current scene.
 991        /// Can detect recently occurred scene changes within recentThreshold seconds.
 992        /// </summary>
 993        /// <param name="seconds">Maximum time to wait in seconds. Default is 30 seconds.</param>
 994        /// <param name="recentThreshold">If scene changed within this many seconds before call, returns immediately. De
 995        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the scene changes.</returns>
 996        /// <exception cref="TimeoutException">Thrown when scene does not change within timeout.</exception>
 997        /// <example>
 998        /// await Click("StartGameButton");
 999        /// await SceneChange(60); // Wait for scene to load (up to 60 seconds)
 1000        /// </example>
 1001        protected async UniTask SceneChange(float seconds = 30, float recentThreshold = 1f)
 01002        {
 01003            string startScene = SceneManager.GetActiveScene().name;
 01004            float startTime = Time.realtimeSinceStartup;
 1005
 01006            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] SceneChange - waiting for scene change from '{startScene}' (timeout: {seconds}s)");
 1007
 01008            float timeSinceLastChange = startTime - lastSceneChangeTime;
 01009            if (timeSinceLastChange < recentThreshold && lastKnownScene != startScene)
 01010            {
 01011                Debug.Log($"[UITEST] SceneChange - scene recently changed ({timeSinceLastChange:F2}s ago) to '{lastKnown
 01012                await ActionComplete();
 01013                return;
 1014            }
 1015
 01016            while ((Time.realtimeSinceStartup - startTime) < seconds && Application.isPlaying && !TestCancellationToken.
 01017            {
 01018                string currentScene = SceneManager.GetActiveScene().name;
 01019                if (currentScene != startScene)
 01020                {
 01021                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST] SceneChange - scene changed to '{currentScene}'");
 01022                    await ActionComplete();
 01023                    return;
 1024                }
 1025
 01026                await UniTask.Delay(EffectiveInterval, true, PlayerLoopTiming.Update, TestCancellationToken);
 01027            }
 1028
 01029            TestCancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
 01030            throw new System.TimeoutException($"Scene did not change from '{startScene}' within {seconds} seconds");
 01031        }
 1032
 1033        /// <summary>
 1034        /// Waits until the game achieves a target framerate. Useful for waiting until loading is complete
 1035        /// or GPU-intensive operations finish. Samples framerate over a duration to get accurate average.
 1036        /// </summary>
 1037        /// <param name="averageFps">The target average FPS to wait for.</param>
 1038        /// <param name="sampleDuration">Duration in seconds to sample framerate. Default is 2 seconds.</param>
 1039        /// <param name="timeout">Maximum time to wait in seconds. Default is 60 seconds.</param>
 1040        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when target framerate is achieved.</returns>
 1041        /// <exception cref="TimeoutException">Thrown when framerate target is not reached within timeout.</exception>
 1042        /// <example>
 1043        /// await Click("LoadLevelButton");
 1044        /// await WaitFramerate(30, 2f, 120); // Wait until 30 FPS sustained for 2 seconds
 1045        /// </example>
 1046        protected async UniTask WaitFramerate(int averageFps, float sampleDuration = 2f, float timeout = 60f)
 01047        {
 01048            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] WaitFramerate - waiting for {averageFps} FPS (sample: {sampleDuration}s, timeout: {time
 1049
 01050            float startTime = Time.realtimeSinceStartup;
 1051
 01052            while ((Time.realtimeSinceStartup - startTime) < timeout && Application.isPlaying && !TestCancellationToken.
 01053            {
 01054                float sampleStart = Time.realtimeSinceStartup;
 01055                int frameCount = 0;
 1056
 01057                while ((Time.realtimeSinceStartup - sampleStart) < sampleDuration && Application.isPlaying && !TestCance
 01058                {
 01059                    await UniTask.Yield(PlayerLoopTiming.Update, TestCancellationToken);
 01060                    frameCount++;
 01061                }
 1062
 01063                float elapsed = Time.realtimeSinceStartup - sampleStart;
 01064                float currentFps = frameCount / elapsed;
 1065
 01066                if (currentFps >= averageFps)
 01067                {
 01068                    Debug.Log($"[UITEST] WaitFramerate - achieved {currentFps:F1} FPS (target: {averageFps})");
 01069                    await ActionComplete();
 01070                    return;
 1071                }
 1072
 01073                Debug.Log($"[UITEST] WaitFramerate - current {currentFps:F1} FPS, waiting for {averageFps}...");
 01074            }
 1075
 01076            TestCancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
 01077            throw new System.TimeoutException($"Framerate did not reach {averageFps} FPS within {timeout} seconds");
 01078        }
 1079
 1080        /// <summary>
 1081        /// Enters text into an input field using realistic input injection.
 1082        /// Clicks the input field to focus it, then types each character.
 1083        /// Supports both TMP_InputField and legacy InputField components.
 1084        /// </summary>
 1085        /// <param name="search">Search query to find the input field.</param>
 1086        /// <param name="input">The text to type into the input field.</param>
 1087        /// <param name="seconds">Maximum time in seconds to search for the input field. Default is 10 seconds.</param>
 1088        /// <param name="pressEnter">If true, presses Enter after typing the text. Useful for submitting forms.</param>
 1089        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when text entry is finished.</returns>
 1090        /// <example>
 1091        /// // Type a username and press Enter to submit
 1092        /// await TextInput(Name("UsernameInput"), "Player123", pressEnter: true);
 1093        ///
 1094        /// // Type into a search field
 1095        /// await TextInput(Adjacent("Search:"), "sword");
 1096        /// </example>
 1097        protected async UniTask TextInput(Search search, string input, float seconds = 10, bool pressEnter = false)
 01098        {
 01099            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] TextInput ({seconds}) '{input}' pressEnter={pressEnter}");
 1100
 1101            // Try to find TMP_InputField or legacy InputField - quick check first (single iteration)
 01102            var findStart = Time.realtimeSinceStartup;
 01103            var tmpInput = await Find<TMP_InputField>(search, false, 0.1f);
 01104            var legacyInputQuick = tmpInput == null ? await Find<InputField>(search, false, 0.1f) : null;
 1105
 1106            // If neither found on quick check, do full timeout search for TMP first, then legacy
 01107            if (tmpInput == null && legacyInputQuick == null)
 01108            {
 01109                Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Quick check failed, doing full {seconds}s search...");
 01110                tmpInput = await Find<TMP_InputField>(search, false, seconds);
 01111            }
 1112
 01113            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Find took {(Time.realtimeSinceStartup - findStart) * 1000:F0}ms, TMP={tmpInput != null}
 1114
 01115            if (tmpInput != null)
 01116            {
 1117                // Click to focus
 01118                await ActionExecutor.ClickAsync(tmpInput.gameObject);
 01119                await UniTask.Yield();
 1120
 1121                // Type characters using ProcessEvent (adds to text) + ForceLabelUpdate (updates display)
 1122                // TMP_InputField uses IMGUI Event.PopEvent() internally which we can't inject into,
 1123                // so we must call ProcessEvent directly and then force the label update
 01124                if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(input))
 01125                {
 01126                    foreach (char c in input)
 01127                    {
 01128                        var keyEvent = new Event
 1129                        {
 1130                            type = EventType.KeyDown,
 1131                            character = c,
 1132                            keyCode = CharToKeyCode(c)
 1133                        };
 01134                        tmpInput.ProcessEvent(keyEvent);
 01135                        tmpInput.ForceLabelUpdate();
 01136                        await UniTask.Yield();
 01137                    }
 01138                }
 1139
 01140                if (pressEnter)
 01141                {
 01142                    var enterEvent = new Event
 1143                    {
 1144                        type = EventType.KeyDown,
 1145                        character = '\n',
 1146                        keyCode = KeyCode.Return
 1147                    };
 01148                    tmpInput.ProcessEvent(enterEvent);
 01149                }
 1150
 01151                await ActionComplete();
 01152                return;
 1153            }
 1154
 1155            // Fall back to legacy InputField (use quick result if found, otherwise full search)
 01156            var legacyInput = legacyInputQuick ?? await Find<InputField>(search, true, seconds);
 1157
 1158            // Click to focus the input field
 01159            await ActionExecutor.ClickAsync(legacyInput.gameObject);
 1160
 1161            // Type characters using ProcessEvent + ForceLabelUpdate
 01162            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(input))
 01163            {
 01164                foreach (char c in input)
 01165                {
 01166                    var keyEvent = new Event
 1167                    {
 1168                        type = EventType.KeyDown,
 1169                        character = c,
 1170                        keyCode = CharToKeyCode(c)
 1171                    };
 01172                    legacyInput.ProcessEvent(keyEvent);
 01173                    legacyInput.ForceLabelUpdate();
 01174                    await UniTask.Yield();
 01175                }
 01176            }
 1177
 01178            if (pressEnter)
 01179            {
 01180                var enterEvent = new Event
 1181                {
 1182                    type = EventType.KeyDown,
 1183                    character = '\n',
 1184                    keyCode = KeyCode.Return
 1185                };
 01186                legacyInput.ProcessEvent(enterEvent);
 01187            }
 1188
 01189            await ActionComplete();
 01190        }
 1191
 1192        /// <summary>
 1193        /// Delegate to get the currently focused object from custom UI systems.
 1194        /// Used by RegisterFocusedObjectGetter to integrate with non-Unity UI systems.
 1195        /// </summary>
 1196        /// <returns>The currently focused GameObject, or null if none.</returns>
 1197        public delegate GameObject FocusedObjectGetter();
 1198
 11199        static readonly List<FocusedObjectGetter> focusedObjectGetters = new();
 1200
 1201        /// <summary>
 1202        /// Registers a getter for the currently focused object in a custom UI system.
 1203        /// Call this to integrate non-Unity UI frameworks (e.g., EzGUI, NGUI) with PressKey methods.
 1204        /// </summary>
 1205        /// <param name="getter">A delegate that returns the currently focused GameObject.</param>
 1206        /// <example>
 1207        /// // Integration with a custom UI system
 1208        /// UITestBehaviour.RegisterFocusedObjectGetter(() => MyUIManager.FocusedObject);
 1209        /// </example>
 1210        public static void RegisterFocusedObjectGetter(FocusedObjectGetter getter)
 01211        {
 01212            if (getter != null && !focusedObjectGetters.Contains(getter))
 01213                focusedObjectGetters.Add(getter);
 01214        }
 1215
 1216        /// <summary>
 1217        /// Simulates a key press and release. Sends the key event to the currently focused/selected UI element.
 1218        /// Works with Unity UI (EventSystem.currentSelectedGameObject) and custom UI systems via registered handlers.
 1219        /// </summary>
 1220        /// <param name="key">The KeyCode to press (e.g., KeyCode.Return, KeyCode.Escape, KeyCode.A).</param>
 1221        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the key press is processed.</returns>
 1222        /// <example>
 1223        /// await Click(Name("InputField")); // Focus input
 1224        /// await PressKey(KeyCode.Return); // Submit
 1225        ///
 1226        /// await PressKey(KeyCode.Escape); // Close dialog
 1227        /// </example>
 1228        protected async UniTask PressKey(KeyCode key)
 01229        {
 01230            GameObject target = GetFocusedObject();
 01231            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] PressKey [{key}] target='{(target != null ? target.name : "none")}'");
 1232
 01233            await PressKeyUnityUI(target, key);
 01234            await ActionComplete();
 01235        }
 1236
 1237        /// <summary>
 1238        /// Simulates a key press on a specific UI element found by search query.
 1239        /// First finds the element, then sends the key event to it.
 1240        /// </summary>
 1241        /// <param name="key">The KeyCode to press.</param>
 1242        /// <param name="search">Search query to find the target element.</param>
 1243        /// <param name="seconds">Maximum time in seconds to search for the element. Default is 10 seconds.</param>
 1244        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the key press is processed.</returns>
 1245        /// <example>
 1246        /// await PressKey(KeyCode.Return, Name("SearchInput")); // Press Enter on search field
 1247        /// </example>
 1248        protected async UniTask PressKey(KeyCode key, Search search, float seconds = 10)
 01249        {
 01250            var component = await Find<Component>(search, true, seconds);
 01251            GameObject target = component?.gameObject;
 01252            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] PressKey [{key}] target='{(target != null ? target.name : "none")}'");
 1253
 01254            await PressKeyUnityUI(target, key);
 01255            await ActionComplete();
 01256        }
 1257
 1258        /// <summary>
 1259        /// Simulates a key press using a character. Automatically converts the character to the appropriate KeyCode.
 1260        /// </summary>
 1261        /// <param name="c">The character to type (e.g., 'a', 'A', '1', ' ').</param>
 1262        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the key press is processed.</returns>
 1263        /// <example>
 1264        /// await PressKey('a'); // Press the 'A' key
 1265        /// await PressKey(' '); // Press spacebar
 1266        /// </example>
 1267        protected async UniTask PressKey(char c)
 01268        {
 01269            var keyCode = CharToKeyCode(c);
 01270            if (keyCode == KeyCode.None)
 01271            {
 01272                Debug.LogWarning($"[UITEST] PressKey - Unable to map character '{c}' to KeyCode");
 01273                return;
 1274            }
 01275            await PressKey(keyCode);
 01276        }
 1277
 1278        /// <summary>
 1279        /// Simulates a key press using a key name string. Supports KeyCode names and common aliases.
 1280        /// </summary>
 1281        /// <param name="keyName">The key name (e.g., "Enter", "Space", "A", "Escape", "up", "down").</param>
 1282        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the key press is processed.</returns>
 1283        /// <example>
 1284        /// await PressKey("Enter"); // Press Enter
 1285        /// await PressKey("Escape"); // Press Escape
 1286        /// await PressKey("up"); // Press Up arrow
 1287        /// await PressKey("A"); // Press A key
 1288        /// </example>
 1289        protected async UniTask PressKey(string keyName)
 01290        {
 1291            // Try single character first
 01292            if (keyName.Length == 1)
 01293            {
 01294                await PressKey(keyName[0]);
 01295                return;
 1296            }
 1297
 1298            // Try parsing as KeyCode name
 01299            if (System.Enum.TryParse<KeyCode>(keyName, true, out var keyCode))
 01300            {
 01301                await PressKey(keyCode);
 01302                return;
 1303            }
 1304
 1305            // Handle common aliases
 01306            var mappedKey = keyName.ToLowerInvariant() switch
 1307            {
 01308                "enter" => KeyCode.Return,
 01309                "esc" => KeyCode.Escape,
 01310                "up" => KeyCode.UpArrow,
 01311                "down" => KeyCode.DownArrow,
 01312                "left" => KeyCode.LeftArrow,
 01313                "right" => KeyCode.RightArrow,
 01314                "bs" or "backspace" => KeyCode.Backspace,
 01315                "del" => KeyCode.Delete,
 01316                _ => KeyCode.None
 1317            };
 1318
 01319            if (mappedKey != KeyCode.None)
 01320            {
 01321                await PressKey(mappedKey);
 01322                return;
 1323            }
 1324
 01325            Debug.LogWarning($"[UITEST] PressKey - Unable to map key name '{keyName}' to KeyCode");
 01326        }
 1327
 1328        /// <summary>
 1329        /// Types a sequence of characters by injecting keyboard text events via the Input System.
 1330        /// Each character is sent as a TextEvent to the currently focused input field.
 1331        /// Use TextInput for a complete input field interaction (click + type).
 1332        /// </summary>
 1333        /// <param name="text">The text string to type, one character at a time.</param>
 1334        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when all characters have been typed.</returns>
 1335        /// <example>
 1336        /// await Click(Name("InputField")); // Focus the input
 1337        /// await PressKeys("Hello World!"); // Type the text
 1338        /// </example>
 1339        protected async UniTask PressKeys(string text)
 01340        {
 01341            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(text))
 01342                return;
 1343
 01344            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] PressKeys - Typing '{text}' ({text.Length} characters)");
 1345
 01346            var keyboard = Keyboard.current;
 01347            if (keyboard == null)
 01348            {
 01349                Debug.LogWarning("[UITEST] PressKeys - No keyboard device found");
 01350                return;
 1351            }
 1352
 1353            // Inject each character as a text event via Input System
 01354            foreach (char c in text)
 01355            {
 01356                var textEvent = TextEvent.Create(keyboard.deviceId, c);
 01357                InputSystem.QueueEvent(ref textEvent);
 01358                await UniTask.Yield();
 01359            }
 01360        }
 1361
 1362        /// <summary>
 1363        /// Maps a character to its corresponding KeyCode.
 1364        /// </summary>
 1365        private static KeyCode CharToKeyCode(char c)
 01366        {
 1367            // Letters (case-insensitive)
 01368            if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z')
 01369                return KeyCode.A + (c - 'a');
 01370            if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z')
 01371                return KeyCode.A + (c - 'A');
 1372
 1373            // Numbers
 01374            if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
 01375                return KeyCode.Alpha0 + (c - '0');
 1376
 1377            // Common symbols
 01378            return c switch
 1379            {
 01380                ' ' => KeyCode.Space,
 01381                '\n' or '\r' => KeyCode.Return,
 01382                '\t' => KeyCode.Tab,
 01383                '\b' => KeyCode.Backspace,
 01384                '`' or '~' => KeyCode.BackQuote,
 01385                '-' or '_' => KeyCode.Minus,
 01386                '=' or '+' => KeyCode.Equals,
 01387                '[' or '{' => KeyCode.LeftBracket,
 01388                ']' or '}' => KeyCode.RightBracket,
 01389                '\\' or '|' => KeyCode.Backslash,
 01390                ';' or ':' => KeyCode.Semicolon,
 01391                '\'' or '"' => KeyCode.Quote,
 01392                ',' or '<' => KeyCode.Comma,
 01393                '.' or '>' => KeyCode.Period,
 01394                '/' or '?' => KeyCode.Slash,
 01395                _ => KeyCode.None
 1396            };
 01397        }
 1398
 1399        private static GameObject GetFocusedObject()
 01400        {
 1401            // Try custom UI systems first (e.g., EzGUI)
 01402            foreach (var getter in focusedObjectGetters)
 01403            {
 01404                var focused = getter();
 01405                if (focused != null)
 01406                    return focused;
 01407            }
 1408            // Fall back to Unity UI EventSystem
 01409            return EventSystem.current?.currentSelectedGameObject;
 01410        }
 1411
 1412        private async UniTask PressKeyUnityUI(GameObject target, KeyCode key)
 01413        {
 1414            // Use the new Input System to inject true key press events
 01415            var keyboard = Keyboard.current;
 01416            if (keyboard == null)
 01417            {
 01418                Debug.LogWarning("[UITEST] PressKey - No keyboard device found, cannot inject key");
 01419                return;
 1420            }
 1421
 01422            var inputKey = KeyCodeToKey(key);
 01423            if (inputKey == Key.None)
 01424            {
 01425                Debug.LogWarning($"[UITEST] PressKey - Unable to map KeyCode.{key} to Input System Key");
 01426                return;
 1427            }
 1428
 1429            // Get the character for this key (for text input)
 01430            char? textChar = KeyCodeToChar(key);
 1431
 1432            // Inject key down
 01433            using (StateEvent.From(keyboard, out var eventPtr))
 01434            {
 01435                keyboard[inputKey].WriteValueIntoEvent(1f, eventPtr);
 01436                InputSystem.QueueEvent(eventPtr);
 01437            }
 1438
 1439            // Also inject a text event if this is a printable character
 1440            // This is required for InputField to receive the character
 01441            if (textChar.HasValue)
 01442            {
 01443                var textEvent = TextEvent.Create(keyboard.deviceId, textChar.Value);
 01444                InputSystem.QueueEvent(ref textEvent);
 01445            }
 1446
 1447            // Wait a frame for the input to be processed
 01448            await UniTask.Yield();
 1449
 1450            // Inject key up
 01451            using (StateEvent.From(keyboard, out var eventPtr))
 01452            {
 01453                keyboard[inputKey].WriteValueIntoEvent(0f, eventPtr);
 01454                InputSystem.QueueEvent(eventPtr);
 01455            }
 01456        }
 1457
 1458        /// <summary>
 1459        /// Maps a KeyCode to its printable character (if applicable).
 1460        /// </summary>
 1461        private static char? KeyCodeToChar(KeyCode keyCode)
 01462        {
 1463            // Letters (lowercase)
 01464            if (keyCode >= KeyCode.A && keyCode <= KeyCode.Z)
 01465                return (char)('a' + (keyCode - KeyCode.A));
 1466
 1467            // Numbers
 01468            if (keyCode >= KeyCode.Alpha0 && keyCode <= KeyCode.Alpha9)
 01469                return (char)('0' + (keyCode - KeyCode.Alpha0));
 1470
 1471            // Common symbols
 01472            return keyCode switch
 1473            {
 01474                KeyCode.Space => ' ',
 01475                KeyCode.BackQuote => '`',
 01476                KeyCode.Minus => '-',
 01477                KeyCode.Equals => '=',
 01478                KeyCode.LeftBracket => '[',
 01479                KeyCode.RightBracket => ']',
 01480                KeyCode.Backslash => '\\',
 01481                KeyCode.Semicolon => ';',
 01482                KeyCode.Quote => '\'',
 01483                KeyCode.Comma => ',',
 01484                KeyCode.Period => '.',
 01485                KeyCode.Slash => '/',
 01486                _ => null
 1487            };
 01488        }
 1489
 1490        /// <summary>
 1491        /// Maps a legacy KeyCode to the new Input System Key.
 1492        /// </summary>
 1493        private static Key KeyCodeToKey(KeyCode keyCode)
 01494        {
 01495            return keyCode switch
 1496            {
 1497                // Letters
 01498                KeyCode.A => Key.A, KeyCode.B => Key.B, KeyCode.C => Key.C, KeyCode.D => Key.D,
 01499                KeyCode.E => Key.E, KeyCode.F => Key.F, KeyCode.G => Key.G, KeyCode.H => Key.H,
 01500                KeyCode.I => Key.I, KeyCode.J => Key.J, KeyCode.K => Key.K, KeyCode.L => Key.L,
 01501                KeyCode.M => Key.M, KeyCode.N => Key.N, KeyCode.O => Key.O, KeyCode.P => Key.P,
 01502                KeyCode.Q => Key.Q, KeyCode.R => Key.R, KeyCode.S => Key.S, KeyCode.T => Key.T,
 01503                KeyCode.U => Key.U, KeyCode.V => Key.V, KeyCode.W => Key.W, KeyCode.X => Key.X,
 01504                KeyCode.Y => Key.Y, KeyCode.Z => Key.Z,
 1505
 1506                // Numbers
 01507                KeyCode.Alpha0 => Key.Digit0, KeyCode.Alpha1 => Key.Digit1, KeyCode.Alpha2 => Key.Digit2,
 01508                KeyCode.Alpha3 => Key.Digit3, KeyCode.Alpha4 => Key.Digit4, KeyCode.Alpha5 => Key.Digit5,
 01509                KeyCode.Alpha6 => Key.Digit6, KeyCode.Alpha7 => Key.Digit7, KeyCode.Alpha8 => Key.Digit8,
 01510                KeyCode.Alpha9 => Key.Digit9,
 1511
 1512                // Numpad
 01513                KeyCode.Keypad0 => Key.Numpad0, KeyCode.Keypad1 => Key.Numpad1, KeyCode.Keypad2 => Key.Numpad2,
 01514                KeyCode.Keypad3 => Key.Numpad3, KeyCode.Keypad4 => Key.Numpad4, KeyCode.Keypad5 => Key.Numpad5,
 01515                KeyCode.Keypad6 => Key.Numpad6, KeyCode.Keypad7 => Key.Numpad7, KeyCode.Keypad8 => Key.Numpad8,
 01516                KeyCode.Keypad9 => Key.Numpad9,
 01517                KeyCode.KeypadDivide => Key.NumpadDivide, KeyCode.KeypadMultiply => Key.NumpadMultiply,
 01518                KeyCode.KeypadMinus => Key.NumpadMinus, KeyCode.KeypadPlus => Key.NumpadPlus,
 01519                KeyCode.KeypadEnter => Key.NumpadEnter, KeyCode.KeypadPeriod => Key.NumpadPeriod,
 1520
 1521                // Function keys
 01522                KeyCode.F1 => Key.F1, KeyCode.F2 => Key.F2, KeyCode.F3 => Key.F3, KeyCode.F4 => Key.F4,
 01523                KeyCode.F5 => Key.F5, KeyCode.F6 => Key.F6, KeyCode.F7 => Key.F7, KeyCode.F8 => Key.F8,
 01524                KeyCode.F9 => Key.F9, KeyCode.F10 => Key.F10, KeyCode.F11 => Key.F11, KeyCode.F12 => Key.F12,
 1525
 1526                // Arrow keys
 01527                KeyCode.UpArrow => Key.UpArrow, KeyCode.DownArrow => Key.DownArrow,
 01528                KeyCode.LeftArrow => Key.LeftArrow, KeyCode.RightArrow => Key.RightArrow,
 1529
 1530                // Special keys
 01531                KeyCode.Space => Key.Space, KeyCode.Return => Key.Enter, KeyCode.Escape => Key.Escape,
 01532                KeyCode.Tab => Key.Tab, KeyCode.Backspace => Key.Backspace, KeyCode.Delete => Key.Delete,
 01533                KeyCode.Insert => Key.Insert, KeyCode.Home => Key.Home, KeyCode.End => Key.End,
 01534                KeyCode.PageUp => Key.PageUp, KeyCode.PageDown => Key.PageDown,
 1535
 1536                // Modifiers
 01537                KeyCode.LeftShift => Key.LeftShift, KeyCode.RightShift => Key.RightShift,
 01538                KeyCode.LeftControl => Key.LeftCtrl, KeyCode.RightControl => Key.RightCtrl,
 01539                KeyCode.LeftAlt => Key.LeftAlt, KeyCode.RightAlt => Key.RightAlt,
 01540                KeyCode.LeftCommand => Key.LeftMeta, KeyCode.RightCommand => Key.RightMeta,
 01541                KeyCode.LeftWindows => Key.LeftWindows, KeyCode.RightWindows => Key.RightWindows,
 1542
 1543                // Symbols
 01544                KeyCode.BackQuote => Key.Backquote, KeyCode.Minus => Key.Minus, KeyCode.Equals => Key.Equals,
 01545                KeyCode.LeftBracket => Key.LeftBracket, KeyCode.RightBracket => Key.RightBracket,
 01546                KeyCode.Backslash => Key.Backslash, KeyCode.Semicolon => Key.Semicolon, KeyCode.Quote => Key.Quote,
 01547                KeyCode.Comma => Key.Comma, KeyCode.Period => Key.Period, KeyCode.Slash => Key.Slash,
 1548
 1549                // Other
 01550                KeyCode.CapsLock => Key.CapsLock, KeyCode.Numlock => Key.NumLock,
 01551                KeyCode.ScrollLock => Key.ScrollLock, KeyCode.Pause => Key.Pause,
 01552                KeyCode.Print => Key.PrintScreen,
 1553
 01554                _ => Key.None
 1555            };
 01556        }
 1557
 1558        #region Key Hold / Sequence
 1559
 1560        /// <summary>
 1561        /// Holds a key down for the specified duration, then releases it.
 1562        /// Useful for movement controls (WASD, arrow keys) or charged attacks.
 1563        /// </summary>
 1564        /// <param name="key">The KeyCode to hold (e.g., KeyCode.W for forward movement).</param>
 1565        /// <param name="duration">How long to hold the key in seconds.</param>
 1566        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the key is released.</returns>
 1567        /// <example>
 1568        /// // Hold W key for 2 seconds (move forward)
 1569        /// await HoldKey(KeyCode.W, 2f);
 1570        ///
 1571        /// // Hold shift for sprint
 1572        /// await HoldKey(KeyCode.LeftShift, 3f);
 1573        /// </example>
 1574        protected async UniTask HoldKey(KeyCode key, float duration)
 01575        {
 01576            var inputKey = KeyCodeToKey(key);
 01577            if (inputKey == Key.None)
 01578            {
 01579                Debug.LogWarning($"[UITEST] HoldKey - Unable to map KeyCode.{key} to Input System Key");
 01580                return;
 1581            }
 01582            await ActionExecutor.HoldKeyAsync(inputKey, duration);
 01583        }
 1584
 1585        /// <summary>
 1586        /// Holds a key down for the specified duration using Input System Key enum.
 1587        /// </summary>
 1588        /// <param name="key">The Input System Key to hold.</param>
 1589        /// <param name="duration">How long to hold the key in seconds.</param>
 1590        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the key is released.</returns>
 1591        /// <example>
 1592        /// await HoldKey(Key.W, 2f); // Move forward for 2 seconds
 1593        /// </example>
 1594        protected async UniTask HoldKey(Key key, float duration)
 01595        {
 01596            await ActionExecutor.HoldKeyAsync(key, duration);
 01597        }
 1598
 1599        /// <summary>
 1600        /// Holds multiple keys down simultaneously for the specified duration.
 1601        /// Useful for diagonal movement (W+A, W+D), sprinting (Shift+W), or key combinations.
 1602        /// </summary>
 1603        /// <param name="duration">How long to hold the keys in seconds.</param>
 1604        /// <param name="keys">The KeyCode values to hold together.</param>
 1605        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when all keys are released.</returns>
 1606        /// <example>
 1607        /// // Diagonal movement (forward-left)
 1608        /// await HoldKeys(2f, KeyCode.W, KeyCode.A);
 1609        ///
 1610        /// // Sprint forward
 1611        /// await HoldKeys(3f, KeyCode.LeftShift, KeyCode.W);
 1612        /// </example>
 1613        protected async UniTask HoldKeys(float duration, params KeyCode[] keys)
 01614        {
 01615            var inputKeys = new Key[keys.Length];
 01616            for (int i = 0; i < keys.Length; i++)
 01617            {
 01618                inputKeys[i] = KeyCodeToKey(keys[i]);
 01619                if (inputKeys[i] == Key.None)
 01620                {
 01621                    Debug.LogWarning($"[UITEST] HoldKeys - Unable to map KeyCode.{keys[i]} to Input System Key");
 01622                    return;
 1623                }
 01624            }
 01625            await ActionExecutor.HoldKeysAsync(inputKeys, duration);
 01626        }
 1627
 1628        /// <summary>
 1629        /// Holds multiple keys down simultaneously for the specified duration using Input System Key enum.
 1630        /// </summary>
 1631        /// <param name="duration">How long to hold the keys in seconds.</param>
 1632        /// <param name="keys">The Input System Key values to hold together.</param>
 1633        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when all keys are released.</returns>
 1634        protected async UniTask HoldKeys(float duration, params Key[] keys)
 01635        {
 01636            await ActionExecutor.HoldKeysAsync(keys, duration);
 01637        }
 1638
 1639        #endregion
 1640
 1641
 1642        /// <summary>
 1643        /// Clicks any one of the elements matching the provided search patterns.
 1644        /// Tries each pattern in order and clicks the first matching element found.
 1645        /// </summary>
 1646        /// <param name="searches">Array of name/text patterns to search for.</param>
 1647        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when an element is clicked.</returns>
 1648        /// <exception cref="TestException">Thrown when no matching element is found within timeout.</exception>
 1649        /// <example>
 1650        /// // Click whichever button appears first
 1651        /// await ClickAny("OK", "Continue", "Next");
 1652        /// </example>
 1653        protected async UniTask ClickAny(params string[] searches)
 01654        {
 01655            await ClickAny(searches, throwIfMissing: true, seconds: 5);
 01656        }
 1657
 1658        /// <summary>
 1659        /// Clicks any one of the elements matching the provided search patterns.
 1660        /// Tries each pattern in order and clicks the first matching element found.
 1661        /// </summary>
 1662        /// <param name="searches">Array of name/text patterns to search for.</param>
 1663        /// <param name="throwIfMissing">If true, throws an exception when no element is found. Default is true.</param>
 1664        /// <param name="seconds">Maximum time in seconds to search. Default is 5 seconds.</param>
 1665        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when an element is clicked.</returns>
 1666        /// <exception cref="TestException">Thrown when no matching element is found and throwIfMissing is true.</except
 1667        protected async UniTask ClickAny(string[] searches, bool throwIfMissing = true, float seconds = 5)
 01668        {
 01669            float startTime = Time.realtimeSinceStartup;
 1670
 01671            while ((Time.realtimeSinceStartup - startTime) < seconds && Application.isPlaying)
 01672            {
 1673                // Try each search pattern
 01674                foreach (var searchPattern in searches)
 01675                {
 01676                    var b = await Find<IPointerClickHandler>(new Search().Any(searchPattern), throwIfMissing: false, sec
 1677
 01678                    if (b != null && b is UnityEngine.Component c)
 01679                    {
 01680                        await ActionExecutor.ClickAsync(c.gameObject);
 01681                        await ActionComplete();
 01682                        return;
 1683                    }
 01684                }
 1685
 01686                await UniTask.Delay(100, true);
 01687            }
 1688
 01689            if (throwIfMissing)
 01690            {
 01691                throw new TestException($"ClickAny on '{string.Join(", ", searches)}' could not find any matching target
 1692            }
 01693        }
 1694
 1695        /// <summary>
 1696        /// Performs a long-press (hold) gesture on an element found by search.
 1697        /// Holds the pointer down for the specified duration, then releases.
 1698        /// </summary>
 1699        /// <param name="search">Search query to find the element to hold.</param>
 1700        /// <param name="seconds">Duration in seconds to hold the element.</param>
 1701        /// <param name="throwIfMissing">If true, throws an exception when element is not found. Default is true.</param
 1702        /// <param name="searchTime">Maximum time in seconds to search for the element. Default is 10 seconds.</param>
 1703        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the hold gesture is finished.</returns>
 1704        /// <exception cref="TestException">Thrown when element is not found and throwIfMissing is true.</exception>
 1705        /// <example>
 1706        /// // Long-press to show context menu
 1707        /// await Hold(Name("InventoryItem"), 1.5f);
 1708        ///
 1709        /// // Hold a button for charged action
 1710        /// await Hold(Name("ChargeButton"), 3f);
 1711        /// </example>
 1712        protected async UniTask Hold(Search search, float seconds, bool throwIfMissing = true, float searchTime = 10)
 01713        {
 01714            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Hold (Search) timeout={searchTime}s for {seconds}s");
 1715
 01716            bool found = await ActionExecutor.HoldAsync(search, seconds, searchTime);
 1717
 01718            if (found)
 01719            {
 01720                await ActionComplete();
 01721            }
 01722            else if (throwIfMissing)
 01723            {
 01724                throw new TestException($"Hold (Search) could not find any matching target within {searchTime}s");
 1725            }
 01726        }
 1727
 1728        /// <summary>
 1729        /// Clicks on a UI element matching the Search query using realistic input injection.
 1730        /// This is the primary click method for UI testing.
 1731        /// </summary>
 1732        /// <param name="search">Search query to find the clickable element. Supports implicit string conversion for tex
 1733        /// <param name="throwIfMissing">If true, throws an exception when element is not found. Default is true.</param
 1734        /// <param name="searchTime">Maximum time in seconds to search for the element. Default is 10 seconds.</param>
 1735        /// <param name="repeat">Number of additional times to repeat the click. 0 = click once, 1 = click twice, etc.</
 1736        /// <param name="index">Index of the element to click when multiple elements match (0-based). Default is 0 (firs
 1737        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the click is performed.</returns>
 1738        /// <exception cref="TestException">Thrown when element is not found and throwIfMissing is true.</exception>
 1739        /// <example>
 1740        /// // Click by text (implicit conversion)
 1741        /// await Click("Play");
 1742        ///
 1743        /// // Click by name pattern
 1744        /// await Click(Name("Button*"));
 1745        ///
 1746        /// // Click with options
 1747        /// await Click(Text("Submit"), throwIfMissing: false, searchTime: 5);
 1748        ///
 1749        /// // Click the second matching element
 1750        /// await Click(Name("Tab*"), index: 1);
 1751        ///
 1752        /// // Triple-click (select all text)
 1753        /// await Click(Name("InputField"), repeat: 2);
 1754        /// </example>
 1755        protected async UniTask Click(Search search, bool throwIfMissing = true, float searchTime = 10, int repeat = 0, 
 01756        {
 1757            do
 01758            {
 01759                string indexInfo = index > 0 ? $" index={index}" : "";
 01760                Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Click (Search) timeout={searchTime}s{indexInfo}");
 1761
 01762                bool found = await ActionExecutor.ClickAsync(search, searchTime, index);
 1763
 01764                if (found)
 01765                {
 01766                    await ActionComplete();
 01767                }
 01768                else if (throwIfMissing)
 01769                {
 01770                    string indexMsg = index > 0 ? $" at index {index}" : "";
 01771                    throw new TestException($"Click (Search){indexMsg} could not find any matching target within {search
 1772                }
 1773
 01774                repeat--;
 01775            }
 01776            while (repeat > 0);
 01777        }
 1778
 1779        /// <summary>
 1780        /// Clicks at the center of the screen. Useful for dismissing dialogs or advancing tutorials.
 1781        /// </summary>
 1782        /// <param name="throwIfMissing">Unused parameter for API consistency.</param>
 1783        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the click is performed.</returns>
 1784        /// <example>

 1788        protected async UniTask Click(bool throwIfMissing = true)

 121790            await ClickAt(0.5f, 0.5f);
 41791        }
 1792
 1793        /// <summary>
 1794        /// Clicks at a screen position specified as percentages (0-1).
 1795        /// Useful for clicking at fixed screen positions regardless of resolution.
 1796        /// </summary>
 1797        /// <param name="xPercent">X position as percentage of screen width (0 = left, 0.5 = center, 1 = right).</param>
 1798        /// <param name="yPercent">Y position as percentage of screen height (0 = bottom, 0.5 = center, 1 = top).</param
 1799        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the click is performed.</returns>
 1800        /// <example>
 1801        /// // Click at screen center
 1802        /// await ClickAt(0.5f, 0.5f);
 1803        ///
 1804        /// // Click in top-right area
 1805        /// await ClickAt(0.9f, 0.9f);
 1806        /// </example>
 1807        protected async UniTask ClickAt(float xPercent, float yPercent)
 41808        {
 41809            Vector2 pos = new Vector2(xPercent * Screen.width, yPercent * Screen.height);
 41810            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] ClickAt ({xPercent:P0}, {yPercent:P0}) at ({pos.x:F0}, {pos.y:F0})");
 1811
 121812            await ActionExecutor.ClickAtAsync(pos);
 121813            await ActionComplete();
 41814        }
 1815

 1889        /// <example>

 01920        protected async UniTask TripleClickAt(float xPercent, float yPercent)

 1929        /// <summary>
 1930        /// Performs a triple-click at screen center.
 1931        /// Performs three rapid clicks in succession.
 1932        /// </summary>

 1943        /// <summary>
 1944        /// Scrolls the mouse wheel over an element found by search.
 1945        /// Uses realistic mouse wheel input injection.
 1946        /// </summary>
 1947        /// <param name="search">Search query to find the element to scroll on.</param>
 1948        /// <param name="delta">Scroll delta (positive = scroll up/zoom in, negative = scroll down/zoom out).</param>
 1949        /// <param name="throwIfMissing">If true, throws an exception when element is not found. Default is true.</param
 1950        /// <param name="searchTime">Maximum time in seconds to search for the element. Default is 10 seconds.</param>
 1951        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the scroll is performed.</returns>
 1952        /// <example>
 1953        /// // Scroll up on a list
 1954        /// await Scroll(Name("ItemList"), 1f);
 1955        ///
 1956        /// // Scroll down
 1957        /// await Scroll(Name("ScrollArea"), -2f);
 1958        /// </example>
 1959        protected async UniTask Scroll(Search search, float delta, bool throwIfMissing = true, float searchTime = 10)
 01960        {
 01961            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] Scroll (Search) timeout={searchTime}s delta={delta}");
 01962
 01963            bool found = await ActionExecutor.ScrollAsync(search, delta, searchTime);
 1964
 01965            if (found)
 01966            {
 01967                await ActionComplete();
 01968            }
 01969            else if (throwIfMissing)
 01970            {
 01971                throw new TestException($"Scroll (Search) could not find any matching target within {searchTime}s");
 01972            }
 01973        }

 02032                _ => Vector2.zero
 2033            };

 32532        /// <summary>
 2533        /// Scrolls a ScrollRect using drag gestures until the target element becomes visible on screen, then returns it
 32534        /// Uses realistic input injection (drag gestures) - does not manipulate scroll position directly.
 2535        /// Ideal for scrolling to items in long lists or grids.
 32536        /// </summary>
 32537        /// <param name="scrollViewSearch">Search query to find the ScrollRect component.</param>
 32538        /// <param name="targetSearch">Search query to find the target element inside the scroll view.</param>
 2539        /// <param name="maxScrollAttempts">Maximum number of scroll attempts before giving up. Default is 20.</param>
 32540        /// <param name="throwIfMissing">If true, throws an exception when elements are not found. Default is true.</par
 32541        /// <param name="searchTime">Initial timeout in seconds for finding elements. Default is 5 seconds.</param>
 2542        /// <returns>The target GameObject once visible, or null if not found.</returns>
 2543        /// <exception cref="TestException">Thrown when elements are not found and throwIfMissing is true.</exception>
 32544        /// <example>
 32545        /// // Scroll to and click an item in a list
 2546        /// var item = await ScrollTo(Name("InventoryList"), Name("SwordItem"));
 112547        /// await Click(item);
 112548        ///
 332549        /// // Scroll to a specific player in a leaderboard
 2550        /// await ScrollTo(Name("LeaderboardScroll"), Text("*Player123*"));
 2551        /// </example>
 112552        protected async UniTask<GameObject> ScrollTo(Search scrollViewSearch, Search targetSearch, int maxScrollAttempts
 6412553        {
 242554            Debug.Log($"[UITEST] ScrollTo - searching for scroll view and target");
 2555
 2556            // Find the ScrollRect
 02557            var scrollRect = await Find<ScrollRect>(scrollViewSearch, throwIfMissing, searchTime);
 02558            if (scrollRect == null) return null;
 522559
 112560            var viewport = scrollRect.viewport ?? scrollRect.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
 112561            var content = scrollRect.content;
 32562
 32563            if (content == null)

 02571            var canvas = scrollRect.GetComponentInParent<Canvas>();

 02574                cam = canvas.worldCamera ?? Camera.main;

 82576            // Calculate scroll distances based on viewport size
 02577            Vector3[] viewportCorners = new Vector3[4];
 82578            viewport.GetWorldCorners(viewportCorners);
 82579
 82580            // For ScreenSpaceOverlay, world corners ARE screen coordinates

 2582            Vector2 viewportMin, viewportMax;
 02583            if (canvas != null && canvas.renderMode == RenderMode.ScreenSpaceOverlay)
 82584            {
 82585                viewportMin = viewportCorners[0];
 02586                viewportMax = viewportCorners[2];
 82587            }
 82588            else
 82589            {
 82590                viewportMin = RectTransformUtility.WorldToScreenPoint(cam, viewportCorners[0]);
 02591                viewportMax = RectTransformUtility.WorldToScreenPoint(cam, viewportCorners[2]);
 82592            }
 82593
 82594            float viewportHeight = Mathf.Abs(viewportMax.y - viewportMin.y);
 82595            float viewportWidth = Mathf.Abs(viewportMax.x - viewportMin.x);
 2596            // Use 40% of the smaller dimension to ensure drag stays within viewport bounds

 82605

 02613

 02629                }

 02635                if (allTargets.Count > 0)
 02636                {

 82662                            }
 82663                            else
 02664                            {

 02676                            }
 2677                            else
 02678                            {
 2679                                // Target is to the left - drag right to scroll content left

 02681                            }

 2737        /// <summary>
 112738        /// Scrolls a ScrollRect until the target element becomes visible, then clicks on it.
 112739        /// Combines ScrollTo and Click into a single convenience method.
 112740        /// </summary>
 112741        /// <param name="scrollViewSearch">Search query to find the ScrollRect component.</param>
 2742        /// <param name="targetSearch">Search query to find the target element to click inside the scroll view.</param>
 112743        /// <param name="maxScrollAttempts">Maximum number of scroll attempts before giving up. Default is 20.</param>
 112744        /// <param name="throwIfMissing">If true, throws an exception when elements are not found. Default is true.</par
 112745        /// <param name="searchTime">Initial timeout in seconds for finding the scroll view. Default is 5 seconds.</para
 112746        /// <returns>A UniTask that completes when the element is scrolled to and clicked.</returns>
 2747        /// <example>
 2748        /// // Scroll to and click an inventory item
 2749        /// await ScrollToAndClick(Name("InventoryList"), Name("SwordItem"));
 112750        ///
 112751        /// // Select a friend from a scrollable list
 2752        /// await ScrollToAndClick(Name("FriendsScroll"), Text("*JohnDoe*"));
 112753        /// </example>
 112754        protected async UniTask ScrollToAndClick(Search scrollViewSearch, Search targetSearch, int maxScrollAttempts = 2
 02755        {
 112756            var target = await ScrollTo(scrollViewSearch, targetSearch, maxScrollAttempts, throwIfMissing, searchTime);
 112757            if (target != null)
 112758            {
 02759                await ActionExecutor.ClickAsync(target);
 112760                await ActionComplete();
 02761            }
 112762        }

 112764        /// <summary>
 112765        /// Checks if a GameObject is a descendant of the given parent transform.
 2766        /// </summary>
 2767        private static bool IsDescendantOf(Transform child, Transform parent)
 02768        {
 02769            Transform current = child;
 02770            while (current != null)
 02771            {
 02772                if (current == parent)
 02773                    return true;
 02774                current = current.parent;
 02775            }
 02776            return false;
 02777        }
